title: Sprites, Groups, Update Contracts, Kill Semantics, and Entity Ownership · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for sprites, groups, update contracts, kill semantics, and entity ownership and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reconciled sprite and group lifecycle. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.
Use separate update, draw-layer, collision, projectile, hazard, and UI groups only when ownership and membership are explicit.
Keep group update inputs consistent or route context through an explicit system boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Sprite Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Sprite Contract Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Sprite Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Sprite Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Sprite Contract, keep Sprite Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 8494 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Layered Groups, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Layered Groups precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Layered Groups, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 9831 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Layered Groups, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Current group iteration order is a stable rendering contract.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Layered Groups for reconciled sprite and group lifecycle.
Run the Layered Groups boundary case for reconciled sprite and group lifecycle, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Layered Groups result because visible reconciled sprite and group lifecycle output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Layered Groups.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Layered Groups
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Layered Groups: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 8212 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Layered Groups.
steps.why: Retrieving Layered Groups through the concrete reconciled sprite and group lifecycle case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Layered Groups, keep Layered Groups connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.
